data.title: Thermo Fisher to sell microbiology unit to Astorg for $1.08B
data.description:
• Thermo Fisher Scientific (TMO) on Monday said it has agreed to sell its microbiology business to private equity firm Astorg for about $1.075 billion in cash and a $50 million seller note.
  • The unit, which provides antimicrobial susceptibility testing and culture media solutions, generated $645 million in revenue in 2025 and is part of Thermo Fisher’s specialty diagnostics segment.
  • The company expects the deal to close in the second half of 2026, subject to regulatory approvals and customary conditions.
  • Thermo Fisher said the transaction is expected to be dilutive to adjusted earnings per share by $0.15 in the first full year after closing.
  • TMO -0.12% premarket to $469.12.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Comfort Systems USA leads all large-cap industrial stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap industrial stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors within industrials, including Construction and Engineering, Aerospace and Defense, Industrial Machinery & Supplies & Components, Electrical Components and Equipment, Heavy Electrical Equipment, and Research and Consulting Services.

Comfort Systems USA (FIX) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 158.63% year-over-year, reflecting aggressive investment in its HVAC and mechanical contracting operations amid booming demand driven by data center and industrial construction.

SPX Technologies (SPXC) and Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 142.37% and 132.94% respectively, while IES Holdings (IESC) and Carpenter Technology (CRS) round out the top five at 94.27% and 77.25%.

Vertiv Holdings (VRT) and MasTec (MTZ) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 76.57% and 74.66%, while GE Vernova (GEV) and Planet Labs (PL) follow at 74.65% and 73.18%.

Axon Enterprise (AXON) rounds out the list at 72.95%.

A standout quant signal emerges from the middle of the rankings: MasTec (MTZ) is the only Strong Buy on the list with a Quant Rating of 4.72, while SPX Technologies (SPXC) carries the sole Buy rating at 4.14, making these two names the most favored by the quant model despite neither leading in raw CapEx growth. The remaining eight stocks all carry Hold ratings ranging from 2.63 to 3.49.

Here is the list:

  1. Comfort Systems USA, Inc. (FIX), CapEx growth YoY: 158.63%

  2. SPX Technologies, Inc. (SPXC), CapEx growth YoY: 142.37%

  3. Rocket Lab Corporation (RKLB), CapEx growth YoY: 132.94%

  4. IES Holdings, Inc. (IESC), CapEx growth YoY: 94.27%

  5. Carpenter Technology Corporation (CRS), CapEx growth YoY: 77.25%

  6. Vertiv Holdings Co (VRT), CapEx growth YoY: 76.57%

  7. MasTec, Inc. (MTZ), CapEx growth YoY: 74.66%

  8. GE Vernova Inc. (GEV), CapEx growth YoY: 74.65%

  9. Planet Labs PBC (PL), CapEx growth YoY: 73.18%

  10. Axon Enterprise, Inc. (AXON), CapEx growth YoY: 72.95%

Industrials ETFs: (XLI), (VIS), (FXR), (IYJ), (FIDU), and (RSPN)

data.title: Youxin Technology jumps over 40% on $10.8M deal for YATOP stake
Dell, HP Enterprise in focus as BofA ups price targets on agentic AI boost
data.description:
Dell Technologies (DELL) and HP Enterprise (HPE) were in focus on Monday as Bank of America upped its price targets on the pair, citing a boost from agentic artificial intelligence.

“The next phase of AI is agentic with multistep workloads requiring more CPU intensive hardware, storage access, and execution systems,” analysts at the firm, led by Wamsi Mohan, wrote in a note to clients. “Agentic AI turns one discrete inferencing event into sequenced workflows, driving more inference events per task, and an increase in persistent workloads driving more overall infrastructure spend in AI servers, ISS, and storage. The importance of agentic AI for Dell and HPE is increasing demand for AI servers and storage but especially their traditional server business. Dell has been selling traditional servers to NeoClouds and we expect the momentum of traditional servers to improve over the next few years.”

Dell's price target was raised to $246 from $205, while HP Enterprise's target was lifted to $38 from $32.

Delving deeper, the analysts said they see Dell and HP Enterprise gaining share in the server market, amid the pickup in demand caused by agentic AI.

“The shift towards agentic AI benefits AI server demand first as inference frequency is increased and model usage becomes a persistent production workload,” the analysts added. “We see Dell as a leading OEM in AI servers, with 12% share of total AI server revenues of $496bn in 2026 while share gain velocity at Neo Clouds is increasing (potentially ~$10bn this year). While HPE is shifting focus towards networking given Juniper (JNPR) acquisition, they still benefit as a premium AI server OEM (we estimate $6.5bn of AI server revs in C26).”

data.title: Real Brokerage confirms RE/MAX acquisition for enterprise value of ~$880M
data.description:
Real Brokerage (REAX) confirmed the acquisition of RE/MAX (RMAX) for an enterprise value of ~$880M.

The enterprise value represents a fully synergized multiple of 7x 2025 EBITDA.

RMAX shares were +17.52% to $9.39 after the announcement, while REAX was 8.58% down to $2.45.

The transaction values each RE/MAX share at $13.80. RE/MAX stockholders can elect to receive 5.15 shares of Real REMAX Group or $13.80 in cash.

The aggregate cash proceeds to RE/MAX investors can be no less than $60M and no greater than $80M.

Real shareholders will receive 1 share of Real REMAX Group for each share they own.

Following the transaction, Real shareholders are expected to own about 59% of the combined company, and RE/MAX investors about 41%.

The two companies are set to form a holding company called Real REMAX Group.

The transaction is expected to be accretive to Real's earnings and adjusted EBITDA margin within the first fiscal year of the close of the transaction.

The combined entity is expected to generate ~$2.3B in annual revenue and $157M in adjusted EBITDA before synergies in 2025.

The transaction is expected to generate about $30M of annual run-rate cost savings.

Bringing two complementary business models together, the acquisition is expected to unite Real's brokerage platform, proprietary software, and agent community with REMAX's real estate brand and expansive global franchise network.

The combined company will have about 8,500 franchisees and 180,000+ agents, with over 100,000 based in the U.S. and Canada.

Real CEO Tamir Poleg will serve as chairman and CEO of the merged global real estate platform, Real REMAX Group. COO Jenna Rozenblat will be the chief integration officer in connection with the transaction.

Real REMAX Group will be headquartered in Miami, with the stock to trade on NASDAQ under the existing ticker REAX.

RE/MAX Co-founder Dave Liniger and certain officers and directors of Real and their affiliated entities have agreed to vote in favor of the transaction.

The transaction is expected to close in the second half.

data.title: OceanFirst, Flushing receive approvals for merger, target June close
data.description:
• OceanFirst Financial (OCFC) and Flushing Financial (FFIC) on Monday said they have received all required regulatory and shareholder approvals to complete their proposed merger.
  • The companies said the U.S. Federal Reserve approved the deal on April 24, following earlier clearances from the New York State Department of Financial Services and the Office of the Comptroller of the Currency.
  • Shareholders of both firms approved the transaction on April 2.
  • OceanFirst and Flushing expect to close the merger by June 1, 2026, subject to remaining customary conditions.
  • Separately, OceanFirst said it will hold its annual shareholder meeting virtually on May 27, 2026.
  • FFIC -0.37% premarket to $16.0.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FirstCash unit launches $600 million senior notes offering
data.description:
• FirstCash Holdings (FCFS) said on Monday its wholly owned subsidiary FirstCash, Inc. has commenced a private placement of $600 million in senior notes due 2034, subject to market conditions.
  • The notes will be unsecured senior obligations and guaranteed by FirstCash and certain domestic subsidiaries.
  • The company plans to use the proceeds to repay a portion of outstanding borrowings under its credit facilities and support future growth.
  • The offering is being made to qualified institutional buyers under Rule 144A and to non-U.S. investors under Regulation S, and the notes have not been registered under U.S. securities laws.
  • The transaction remains subject to market conditions, and there is no assurance it will be completed.
  • FCFS +0.01% after hours to $219.03.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: International Paper Company leads all large-cap material stocks in YoY CapEx growth
data.description:
A rising capital expenditure figure often signals that a company is betting heavily on its own future growth. Below is a list of U.S. large-cap material stocks ranked by their year-over-year CapEx growth percentage, spanning multiple sectors and market capitalizations.

The list spans multiple sub-sectors within materials, including Gold, Silver, Copper, Fertilizers and Agricultural Chemicals, Specialty Chemicals, and Paper & Plastic Packaging Products & Materials.

International Paper (IP) tops the list with a CapEx growth rate of 101.63% year-over-year, reflecting the company's significant investment in manufacturing modernization and capacity expansion across its packaging operations.

CF Industries Holdings (CF) and AngloGold Ashanti (AU) follow in second and third place with growth rates of 83.40% and 33.06% respectively, while Southern Copper Corporation (SCCO) and International Flavors & Fragrances (IFF) round out the top five at 29.01% and 28.29%.

Packaging Corporation of America (PKG) and Coeur Mining (CDE) occupy the sixth and seventh positions with CapEx growth of 23.77% and 20.73%, while Hecla Mining (HL) and DuPont de Nemours (DD) follow at 17.67% and 16.84%.

Solstice Advanced Materials (SOLS) rounds out the list at 13.51%. A key quant signal stands out: AngloGold Ashanti (AU), Southern Copper (SCCO), and Coeur Mining (CDE) are the only three names to carry Buy ratings, at 4.11, 4.34, and 4.01 respectively, suggesting the quant models favor the pure-play miners over the chemicals and packaging names at the top of the list.

Here is the list:

  1. International Paper Company (IP), CapEx growth YoY: 101.63%

  2. CF Industries Holdings, Inc. (CF), CapEx growth YoY: 83.40%

  3. AngloGold Ashanti plc (AU), CapEx growth YoY: 33.06%

  4. Southern Copper Corporation (SCCO), CapEx growth YoY: 29.01%

  5. International Flavors & Fragrances Inc. (IFF), CapEx growth YoY: 28.29%

  6. Packaging Corporation of America (PKG), CapEx growth YoY: 23.77%

  7. Coeur Mining, Inc. (CDE), CapEx growth YoY: 20.73%

  8. Hecla Mining Company (HL), CapEx growth YoY: 17.67%

  9. DuPont de Nemours, Inc. (DD), CapEx growth YoY: 16.84%

  10. Solstice Advanced Materials, Inc. (SOLS), CapEx growth YoY: 13.51%

Materials ETFs: (XLB), (VAW), (IYM), (FXZ), (MXI), and (RSPM)

data.title: U.S. Energy surges after signing five-year helium supply deal for Montana plant
data.description:
U.S. Energy (USEG) up 28.3% pre-market Monday after saying it signed a five-year helium sales agreement with an unnamed "global, investment-grade industrial gas company" for the sale of contained helium to be produced at its Big Sky Carbon Hub in Montana.

U.S. Energy (USEG) said the agreement establishes long-term contracted cash flow to support Phase 1 commercial operations, which remain targeted for Q1 2027.

The deal calls for fully contracted Phase 1 helium volumes of as much as 1.2M cf/month at fixed pricing of $285/Mcf of contained helium on an all-in plant-gate basis, the company said.

"This contract establishes long-term, contracted helium revenues and meaningfully de-risks Phase 1 commercial operations at Big Sky," U.S. Energy (USEG) President and CEO Ryan Smith said. "It also reflects the strength we’re seeing in the helium market today, where constrained global supply and increasing demand for reliable volumes are supporting a step up in long-term pricing."

data.title: Verizon's promos lead to 55K new wireless subscribers; raises FY26 profit guidance
data.description:
Verizon Communications (VZ) is propped up in early Monday trade as first-quarter results  reflected better-than-expected profits and postpaid phone additions, underscoring the company’s successful efforts to lure in new wireless customers despite intense competition from rivals AT&T (T) and T-Mobile (TMUS).

Shares last traded more than 4% higher in premarket.

The 55K increase in new wireless subscribers—a first for Q1—and 341K broadband net additions led Verizon (VZ) to anticipate total retail postpaid phone net additions for FY26 to be at the upper end of its forecast of 750K to 1M, and profits to be within a range of $4.95 to $4.99 per share, representing growth of 5% to 6% and exceeding the $4.91 consensus forecast.  This compares to prior guidance of $4.90 to $4.95 per share.

For FY26, total mobility and broadband service revenue is expected to increase by 2% to 3% to $93B, while wireless revenue is expected to be flat.

For the first quarter, Verizon (VZ) earned an adjusted profit of $1.28 per share, up 7.6% from the same quarter last year, $0.07 better than anticipated, and the best quarterly growth rate since 2021. 

Led by gains in mobility and broadband, total revenue increased 2.7% to $34.4B but missed expectations by $490M as the January service outage shaved 80 basis points off Verizon's (VZ) revenue growth rate for the quarter. 

On the balance sheet, cash flow from operating activities increased by 2.6% to $8.0B, while free cash flow increased by 4% to $3.6B. However, increased spending on promotions raised the company’s debt load, resulting in an unsecured debt-to-consolidated net income ratio of 8x versus 7.4x in the prior quarter.
